title: Senior Backend Engineer - Audiobooks
score_reasoning: Your distributed systems, microservices, and high-scale backend expertise (Python, Go, AWS, observability) align well with ingesting, processing, and transforming content at scale. The 'Senior' title matches your current level, and the responsibilities — owning critical pipeline infrastructure and cross-stack problem-solving — mirror your proven scope at Velox (multi-tenant architecture, zero-downtime migrations, observability leadership). London is geographically closer than New York (roughly 1-2 hour overlap with Berlin) and the role is distributed across Europe, suggesting flexibility. The full-stack-capable, ambiguous-problem-solving scope aligns with your systems-thinking track record. The audiobook domain and emerging AI infrastructure offer legitimate growth opportunities. However, the posting does not explicitly state remote eligibility — while distributed teams often support remote work, London-based positioning without clarity creates moderate uncertainty. Also note: data engineering is a growth area for you, and this role emphasizes data-focused development, which is favorable but not a core strength.

red_flags: Location listed as London (not explicitly remote); distributed team suggests flexibility but is not confirmed
Data engineering emphasis (not a core strength, though listed as secondary in role scope)
Audiobook domain unfamiliar (minor, as domain knowledge is secondary to backend skills)

description:
Spotify’s Audiobooks Mission is transforming how creators and listeners experience long-form storytelling. We’re building the foundation that enables audiobook content to flow seamlessly from publishers to listeners — including the systems that power ingestion, processing, and delivery across a rapidly growing global catalog.
 
We’re looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join a small, high-impact team within the Audiobooks Pipeline area. This team is responsible for building and scaling systems that ingest, process, and transform audiobook and eBook content, metadata, and royalties — ensuring the right content reaches listeners efficiently and with the highest quality.
 
While this role is full stack in scope, the majority of work will lean toward backend and data-focused development. We’re looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, loves to learn, and is eager to stretch beyond their comfort zone to solve challenging problems across the stack.
